data.title: Second Weekly Loss Looms for New Zealand Equities
data.description:
New Zealand shares fell 144 points, or 1.1%, to 13,295 in Friday morning trade, erasing prior gains and approaching a one-month low.

Sentiment soured after Wall Street’s slump on Thursday, driven by renewed fears of overheated AI valuations and fading hopes for a Fed rate cut in December.

The NZX is on track for a second consecutive weekly loss, down roughly 1.3%, as investors adopt a cautious stance ahead of next week’s RBNZ decision, despite expectations for further easing to bolster growth.

Losses were partially offset by October’s narrower trade deficit, with exports outpacing imports.

Most sectors declined, led by Gentrack Group (-3.1%), Infratil (-2.9%), and Ryman Healthcare (-2.8%).

data.title: TGF: Strong returns, board approvals, and strategic focus on critical minerals and capital management
data.description:
AGM highlighted strong 34% CYTD returns, a narrowed NTA discount, and a 5c fully franked dividend. Shareholders approved all resolutions, including board elections. Strategic focus remains on critical minerals, electrification, and capital management initiatives.

Original document: Tribeca Global Natural Resources Ltd. [TGF] Slides Release — Nov. 20 2025

data.title: CARIBBEAN UTILITIES COMPANY, LTD ANNOUNCES DECLARATION OF DIVIDEND ON CLASS A ORDINARY SHARES
data.description:
CARIBBEAN UTILITIES COMPANY, LTD. CLASS A ORDINARY SHARES ARE LISTED FOR TRADING IN UNITED STATES FUNDS ON THE TORONTO STOCK EXCHANGE.

GRAND CAYMAN, Cayman Islands, Nov. 20, 2025 /CNW/ - Caribbean Utilities Company, Ltd. ("CUC" or "the Company") announced that the Board of Directors has declared a dividend of US$0.19 per Class A Ordinary Share, or an annualized dividend of US$0.76 per share. The dividend will be payable on December 15, 2025 to shareholders of record on December 1, 2025.

The principal activity of the Company is to generate, transmit and distribute electricity in its licence area of Grand Cayman, Cayman Islands pursuant to a 20-year Transmission & Distribution ("T&D") Licence and a 25-year non-exclusive Generation Licence (the "Generation Licence" and together with the T&D licence, the "Licences") granted by the Cayman Islands Government (the "Government", "CIG"). The T&D Licence, which expires in April 2028, contains provisions for an automatic 20-year renewal and the Company has reasonable expectation of renewal until April 2048. The Generation Licence expires in November 2039. Further information is available at www.cuc-cayman.com.

Caribbean Utilities Company, Ltd. ("CUC" or "the Company"), on occasion, includes forward-looking statements in its media releases, Canadian securities regulatory authorities filings, shareholder reports and other communications. Forward-looking statements include statements that are predictive in nature, depend upon future events or conditions, or include words such as "expects", "anticipates", "plan", "believes", "estimates", "intends", "targets", "projects", "forecasts", "schedule", or negative versions thereof and other similar expressions, or future or conditional verbs such as "may", "will", "should", "would" and "could". Forward-looking statements are based on underlying assumptions and management's beliefs, estimates and opinions, and are subject to certain risks and uncertainties surrounding future expectations generally that may cause actual results to vary from plans, targets and estimates. Such risks and uncertainties include but are not limited to operational, general economic, market and business conditions, regulatory developments and weather conditions. CUC cautions readers that actual results may vary significantly from those expected should certain risks or uncertainties materialize or should underlying assumptions prove incorrect. Forward-looking statements are provided for the purpose of providing information about management's current expectations and plans relating to the future. Readers are cautioned that such information may not be appropriate for other purposes. The Company disclaims any intention or obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise except as required by law.

SOURCE Caribbean Utilities Company, Ltd.

data.title: PLATTS: 1500--US FERC predicts increased gas prices, production heading into winter
data.description: (Platts)--20Nov2025/554 pm EST/2254 GMT **Gas demand exceeds production **Markets add renewables, retire fossil fuels **EIA projects increased US winter energy consumption The US Federal Energy Regulatory Commission staff projected higher domestic natural gas prices heading into the winter season, particularly in New England and the Northeast, and even amid increased production. In the commission's winter market reliability report released Nov. 20, FERC staff said that, while they projected all grid operators to maintain reliability during normal conditions, an extended cold snap could stress infrastructure and threaten reliability in certain regions of the US. "We're seeing demand growth exceeding new additions at a two-to-one ratio, and that's not sustainable," commissioner David Rosner said during FERC's Nov. 20 open meeting. "This commission has been looking at ways to do things faster ... we need to do more because it takes too long to build in this country." GAS PREDICTIONS Staff said prices were projected to be higher year over year at nearly every trading hub this winter. Prices at the Henry Hub were expected to increase 26% from the winter 2024-25 settled price, up to $4.39/MMBtu, staff said. They cited rising gas demand in the South-Central region as contributing to higher prices at the regional hub pricing benchmark. In New England, where gas constraints often drive higher costs, the Algonquin city-gates hub near Boston could see some of the highest prices in the country, staff said during the open meeting. Staff has forecast an average Algonquin city-gates price of $12.76/MMBtu, an increase of 47 cents from last season's average, according to the report. Staff predicted that total gas demand would exceed production this winter, driven by net exports. Natural gas production is projected to increase 2.9% from last season to an average 108.1 Bcf/d, staff said, citing data from the US Energy Information Administration. Demand for gas is projected to drop 1.6% to 127.6 Bcf/d for the winter season. The decrease in demand is driven in part by warmer-than-average weather conditions projected by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, staff said. "EIA forecasts the residential and commercial sector to consume 41.6 Bcf/d, a decrease of 10.2% from the notably high demand seen during winter 2024-2025 due to the January 2025 winter events," staff said in the report. "Lower residential and commercial demand usually reflects a warmer winter, resulting in lower demand for natural gas used for space heating." Gas inventory levels are relatively consistent compared to the same period of 2024, and the EIA is projecting withdrawals totaling about 1,980 Bcf for the 2025-26 season, a 13% drop from the prior year. Gross LNG exports are also expected to increase, by 18.7% to 16.3 Bcf/d during the winter season. POWER MARKETS The EIA projected that energy consumption this winter will hit 1,035 TWh, a 2.7% increase from the five-year average. Staff cited the North American Electric Reliability Corp.'s 2025 winter report, which estimated that net internal demand for electricity will increase by 1.6% from last year to 677 GW. That report, released Nov. 18, estimated that peak demand would increase by 2.5%. NERC said that the Western Electricity Coordinating Council's Northwest and Basin subregions, as well as the Electric Reliability Council of Texas, parts of the US Southeast, and Northeast, and the Canadian Maritime Provinces, were at an increased risk of supply shortfalls during extreme weather events. US electricity markets have added 64.7 GW of winter capacity since last season, offsetting 8.6 GW of retirements, FERC staff said during the meeting. According to the staff report, 80% of the new capacity additions were in solar and batteries, while 88% of the retirements were in coal and gas. The majority of capacity additions are in ERCOT and WECC, with completed and expected retirements of about 21.9 GW and 14.1 GW of generation, respectively. The New York ISO and ISO New England added the least amount of capacity from last winter, adding just 0.7 and 1.3 GW, respectively. The Midcontinent ISO and WECC lead retirements, with WECC losing 3.7 GW of generation and MISO retiring 2.6 GW. "Projected additions for 2026 are 64.7 GW, which would represent the largest annual capacity addition in over a decade," staff said in the report. "Conversely, the pace of generation retirements has slowed significantly following a surge in coal plant closures." Staff also said that the US was projected to add 19,008 line-miles of new transmission compared to last winter, with projects primarily in ERCOT, MISO and the PJM Interconnection. These additional projects are primarily designed to increase resilience to fire and extreme weather, staff said. — George Weykamp, george.weykamp@spglobal.com-- Edited by Richard Rubin, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: Community Collaboration in El Paso Brings Free Home Repairs to Those in Need
data.description:
NORTHAMPTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / November 20, 2025 / SHEBuilds graduates hold certificates that represent their completion of the program's training modules.

Key points

  • Rebuilding Together El Paso (RTEP) is strengthening its community outreach through its SHEBuilds training program that provides home repair skills.

  • Program participants are being asked to reinforce their skills by devoting time to the nonprofit organization's free repair projects for elderly, disabled and low-income homeowners.

  • Marathon Petroleum's El Paso, Texas, refinery is supporting this effort by extending its financial assistance of RTEP to include the training program.

For 30 years, elderly, disabled and low-income homeowners in El Paso, Texas, have benefited from free, safety and health-related home repairs and improvements that Rebuilding Together El Paso (RTEP) coordinates with contractors and volunteers. This year, the nonprofit organization has been working to further enhance its outreach to another segment of the community: women-led households.

"Our SHEBuilds training program helps those who may not have had experience with home repairs, such as single parents, widows and military spouses eager to learn hands-on skills," said RTEP Executive Director Roger de Moor. "The aim is to equip them to maintain their homes and avoid costly repairs, and potentially prepare them for careers in the home repair and construction industries to enhance their economic stability."

More than 250 trainees have completed the program since it began in 2021. Its eight training modules address electrical work, plumbing, drywall, saving energy, interior painting and installing ceramic tile and grab bars. For 2025, RTEP added a new expectation: Participants are asked to give back to the community by working on RTEP's home repair projects with volunteers from RTEP supporters like Marathon Petroleum Corporation's (MPC) El Paso refinery.

"Recently, we had a group of program participants get trained on installing floor tile and then install tile at a home near the refinery where we were working," said MPC Refining Maintenance Director Brandon Bielamowicz, who organizes the refinery's employee volunteer teams for RTEP projects and serves as the nonprofit's board president. "MPC is also covering the cost of training courses for those that cannot afford it otherwise."

The El Paso refinery is completing its fourth year of providing financial and volunteer assistance to RTEP, which has made dozens of projects possible. This year's grant was $75,000.

"MPC has been a vital supporter of our core home repair program and, by helping the training program, is extending work by skilled volunteers into the MPC home projects," de Moor said. "This collaboration further underscores the program's real-world impact and community alignment."

Bielamowicz noted that it never takes long to line up MPC volunteers to help RTEP.

"I love the engagement and enthusiasm of the El Paso refinery employees, as I frequently get asked when the next project will be," he said. "Anyone who knows me knows that I love working with my hands, so doing projects with Rebuilding Together has been a good mix for me."

View additional multimedia and more ESG storytelling from Marathon Petroleum on 3blmedia.com.

Contact Info:

Spokesperson: Marathon Petroleum

Website: https://www.3blmedia.com/profiles/marathon-petroleum-corporation

Email: info@3blmedia.com

SOURCE: Marathon Petroleum

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Gas utility UGI fiscal 2025 adjusted EPS beats expectations, helped by utilities segment
data.description:
Overview

  • UGI fiscal 2025 adjusted EPS beats analyst expectations

  • UGI fiscal 2025 revenue missed analyst expectations

Outlook

  • UGI projects fiscal 2026 adjusted EPS between $2.90 and $3.15

  • UGI extends EPS CAGR to 5-7% between FY24 and FY29

Result Drivers

  • AMERIGAS IMPROVEMENTS - Higher EBIT at AmeriGas contributed to strong fiscal 2025 results, according to CEO Robert Flexon

  • UTILITIES PERFORMANCE - Solid operational performance from the Utilities segment supported fiscal 2025 results

  • TAX BENEFITS - Significant tax benefits were a key driver of UGI's exceptional fiscal 2025 results

Key Details

Metric

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

FY Revenue

Miss

$7.29 bln

$7.54 bln (2 Analysts)

FY Adjusted EPS

Beat

$3.32

$3.18 (3 Analysts)

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "strong buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy", no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the natural gas utilities peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for UGI Corp is $41.00, about 14.3% above its November 20 closing price of $35.13

  • The stock recently traded at 11 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 11 three months ago

Press Release:

data.title: CFO Hettrich Surrenders 79,429 Of QuantumScape Corp [QS]
data.description:
Hettrich Kevin Chief Financial Officer of QuantumScape Corp [QS] filed a Form 4 on November 20, 2025 with the Securities and Exchange Commission disclosing the following changes in ownership:

Date11/18/25
TransactionSurrender(1)
Shares79,429
Price$13.05
Value$1,036,302

Ending Holdings: 1,512,038 - Direct

  • (1)
  • Surrender of shares back to company to pay option exercise cost and/or resulting tax liability.
